ex.p: How do you say 'I come from France'?
ex.e: Use kommen aus + country. Ech kommen aus Frankräich.

ex.p: What is the feminine form of 'I am Luxembourgish'?
ex.e: Feminine nationality adds -in: Lëtzebuergerin.

ex.p: Complete: Ech wunnen ___ Lëtzebuerg. (I live in Luxembourg.)
ex.e: Exception: use zu (not an) for Luxembourg.

ex.p: How do you form language names in Luxembourgish?
ex.e: Languages use -(e)sch: Lëtzebuergesch, Englesch, Franséisch.

ex.p: What preposition is used with kommen for countries?
ex.e: Ech kommen aus + country. Exception: vu Lëtzebuerg.

ex.p: What does 'Ech si Lëtzebuergesch Proff' mean?
ex.e: Proff = teacher. No article before professions in Luxembourgish.

ex.p: Complete: Ech kommen aus England ___ London.
ex.e: Use vu(n) for cities: Ech kommen aus England vu London.

ex.p: What is the conjugation of kommen with du?
ex.e: Kommen is irregular: ech kommen, du kënns, hien kënnt.

ex.p: What is the past participle of schwätzen?
ex.e: Schwätzen participle is geschwat.

ex.p: How do you say 'I am Italian' (woman)?
ex.e: Feminine: add -in. Italiener -> Italienerin.

ex.p: How do you say 'My name is Anne'?
ex.e: Mäin Numm ass... = My name is... (formal).

ex.p: Complete: Ech schwätze ___. (I speak Luxembourgish.)
ex.e: Lëtzebuergesch is the language name.

ex.p: What does 'an zwar' mean?
ex.e: An zwar = more precisely / to be exact.

ex.p: How do you say 'I like red wine' using gefalen?
ex.e: Gefalen = to please: De roude Wäi gefält mir.

ex.p: What does 'Ech hunn gär Schokolaad' mean?
ex.e: Hunn gär + noun = to like. Ech hunn gär Schokolaad.

ex.p: Complete: Mir ___ de Kaffi net. (I don't like the coffee.)
ex.e: Mir gefält... net = I don't like...

ex.p: How do you ask 'Did you like the food?'
ex.e: Schmäichten for food: Huet iech d'Iessen geschmaacht?

ex.p: What does 'Mäi Lieblingsessen' mean?
ex.e: Lieblings- = favourite, Essen = food.

ex.p: How do you say 'I don't like football at all'?
ex.e: Guer net = not at all.

ex.p: Complete: Ech hunn ___ gär Musek. (I also like music.)
ex.e: Och = also/too.

ex.p: What does 'besonnesch' mean?
ex.e: Besonnesch = especially.

ex.p: What is 'Judd mat Gaardebounen'?
ex.e: Traditional Luxembourgish dish.

ex.p: Complete: Ech spillen Klavier ___ 10 Joer.
ex.e: Zanter = since/for (duration).

ex.p: How do you ask 'What do you like?' (informal)?
ex.e: Wat hues du gär? = What do you like?

ex.p: Complete: Ech hunn net gär fréi ___. (I don't like getting up early.)
ex.e: Opstoen = to get up.

ex.p: What does 'Kachen' mean?
ex.e: Kachen = cooking/to cook.
